# Coding Plan for openclaw/openclaw#83134: Auto-generated VAPID keys use @localhost subject, breaking Apple Web Push (iOS PWA)

Source report: [records/openclaw-openclaw/items/83134.md](records/openclaw-openclaw/items/83134.md)

## Summary

Keep open: current main and the latest shipped release still default Web Push VAPID to `mailto:openclaw@localhost`, the send path passes that subject to `web-push`, and no duplicate fix PR or canonical issue currently owns the repair.

## Plan

Fix https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/83134 by changing the Web Push VAPID fallback subject away from `mailto:openclaw@localhost` to a stable valid OpenClaw-owned `https:` or `mailto:` URI. Preserve `OPENCLAW_VAPID_SUBJECT` precedence and existing keypair behavior so users do not have to delete `push/vapid-keys.json`; do not add a new `openclaw.json` config key unless a maintainer explicitly asks. Update focused tests and Control UI docs, and add a user-facing `CHANGELOG.md` fix entry if policy expects it for this release-facing push bug.

## Likely Files

- `src/infra/push-web.ts`
- `src/infra/push-web.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-methods/push.test.ts`
- `docs/web/control-ui.md`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
